reasoning: The market probability was 0.06 on October 30, 2025. At this late stage in the Formula 1 season (November 9), there are typically only 3 to 4 races remaining. For Hamilton's probability of beating Leclerc to be this low, Leclerc must have a substantial points advantage, meaning Hamilton would need an extreme run of luck (multiple Leclerc DNFs) to catch up. Absent specific information about the most recent race in early November, relying on the highly efficient late-season prediction market is the optimal strategy.
